description: Higher-level threading interface built on top of the thread module.

functions.name: active_count

returns.description: The number of Thread objects currently alive.

functions.description: Return the number of Thread objects currently alive. Equal to the length of the list returned by enumerate().

functions.name: current_thread

returns.description: The current Thread object corresponding to the caller's thread of control.

functions.description: Return the current Thread object corresponding to the caller's thread of control.

functions.name: enumerate

returns.description: A list of all Thread objects currently alive.

functions.description: Return a list of all Thread objects currently alive. Includes daemonic threads, the main thread, and dummy thread objects created by current_thread().

functions.name: settrace

params.name: func
params.description: A trace function to set for all threads. The function is passed to sys.settrace() for each thread before its run() method is called.

functions.description: Set a trace function for all threads started from the threading module. The func is passed to sys.settrace() for each thread before run() is called.

functions.name: setprofile

params.name: func
params.description: A profile function to set for all threads. The function is passed to sys.setprofile() for each thread before its run() method is called.

functions.description: Set a profile function for all threads started from the threading module. The func is passed to sys.setprofile() for each thread before run() is called.

functions.name: stack_size

params.name: size
params.description: The stack size in bytes for new threads. 0 uses the platform default. Must be at least 32768 (32 KiB) if set.

returns.description: The stack size used for newly created threads (0 means platform default).

functions.description: Return the thread stack size used when creating new threads. Optionally set the stack size for subsequently created threads.

classes.name: Thread
classes.description: A class that represents a thread of control. Threads can be created by passing a callable to the constructor or by overriding the run() method in a subclass.

params.name: group
params.description: Reserved for future extension. Must be None.

params.name: target
params.description: The callable object to be invoked by the run() method. Defaults to None.

params.name: name
params.description: The thread name. By default, a unique name of the form 'Thread-N' is assigned.

params.name: args
params.description: The argument tuple for the target invocation. Defaults to ().

params.name: kwargs
params.description: A dictionary of keyword arguments for the target invocation. Defaults to {}.

methods.name: start
methods.description: Start the thread's activity. It must be called at most once per thread object. It arranges for the object's run() method to be invoked in a separate thread of control.

methods.name: run
methods.description: Method representing the thread's activity. Override this method in a subclass. The standard run() method invokes the callable object passed to the constructor.

methods.name: join

params.name: timeout
params.description: Time in seconds to wait. None means wait indefinitely. Defaults to None.

methods.description: Wait until the thread terminates. Blocks the calling thread until the thread whose join() method is called terminates or the timeout occurs.

methods.name: is_alive

returns.description: True if the thread is alive.

methods.description: Return whether the thread is alive. A thread is alive from the moment start() is called until its run() method terminates.

methods.name: getName
methods.description: Return the thread's name. Equivalent to accessing the name attribute.

methods.name: setName

params.name: name
params.description: The new thread name.

methods.description: Set the thread's name. Equivalent to setting the name attribute.

methods.name: setDaemon

params.name: daemonic
params.description: Whether the thread is a daemon thread.

methods.description: Set whether the thread is a daemon thread. Must be called before start(). The entire Python program exits when only daemon threads are left.

methods.name: isDaemon

returns.description: True if the thread is a daemon thread.

methods.description: Return whether the thread is a daemon thread. Equivalent to accessing the daemon attribute.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The thread's name. Multiple threads may share the same name.

fields.name: daemon
fields.description: Whether the thread is a daemon thread. Must be set before start() is called. The initial value is inherited from the creating thread.

fields.name: ident
fields.description: The thread identifier, or None if the thread has not been started. This is a nonzero integer. Thread identifiers may be recycled.

classes.name: Lock
classes.description: A primitive lock object. Once a thread has acquired a lock, subsequent attempts to acquire it block until it is released. Any thread may release it.

constructors.description: Create a new Lock object in the unlocked state.

methods.name: acquire

params.name: blocking
params.description: If True (default), block until the lock is acquired. If False, return immediately.

returns.description: True if the lock is acquired, False otherwise.

methods.description: Acquire the lock, blocking or non-blocking. Returns True if the lock was acquired.

methods.name: release
methods.description: Release the lock. Can be called from any thread, not only the thread that acquired it. Raises ThreadError if called on an unlocked lock.

methods.name: locked

returns.description: True if the lock is currently acquired.

methods.description: Return True if the lock is currently acquired.

classes.name: RLock
classes.description: A reentrant lock object. A reentrant lock can be acquired multiple times by the same thread without blocking. It must be released the same number of times.

constructors.description: Create a new reentrant lock object.

methods.name: acquire

params.name: blocking
params.description: If True (default), block until the lock is acquired. If False, return immediately.

returns.description: True if the lock is acquired, False otherwise.

methods.description: Acquire the lock, blocking or non-blocking. When invoked without arguments, block until the lock is acquired. If the same thread has already acquired the lock, increment the recursion level by one.

methods.name: release
methods.description: Release the lock, decrementing the recursion level. If the recursion level reaches zero, the lock is actually released. Only the owning thread can release the lock.

classes.name: Condition
classes.description: A condition variable allows one or more threads to wait until they are notified by another thread. Always associated with a lock.

params.name: lock
params.description: The underlying lock object. If None (default), a new RLock is created.

constructors.description: Create a condition variable with an optional underlying lock.

methods.name: acquire

params.name: args
params.description: Arguments passed to the underlying lock's acquire() method.

returns.description: The return value of the underlying lock's acquire().

methods.description: Acquire the underlying lock.

methods.name: release
methods.description: Release the underlying lock.

methods.name: wait

params.name: timeout
params.description: Maximum time to wait in seconds. None means wait indefinitely.

returns.description: True unless the timeout expired (always True in Python 2.7).

methods.description: Wait until notified or until a timeout occurs. The calling thread must have acquired the lock. Releases the lock, blocks until notified, then re-acquires the lock.

methods.name: notify

params.name: n
params.description: Number of waiting threads to notify. Defaults to 1.

methods.description: Wake up one or more threads waiting on this condition. The calling thread must have acquired the lock.

methods.name: notify_all
methods.description: Wake up all threads waiting on this condition. The calling thread must have acquired the lock.

classes.name: Event
classes.description: A class that implements event objects. An event manages a flag that can be set to true with the set() method and reset to false with the clear() method. The wait() method blocks until the flag is true.

constructors.description: Create a new Event object with the internal flag set to False.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Set the internal flag to True. All threads waiting for the event are awakened.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Reset the internal flag to False. Threads calling wait() will block until set() is called.

methods.name: wait

params.name: timeout
params.description: Maximum time to wait in seconds. None means wait indefinitely.

returns.description: True if and only if the internal flag has been set to True, either before the wait or after the timeout.

methods.description: Block until the internal flag is true. If the flag is already true, return immediately. Otherwise, block until another thread calls set() or until the timeout occurs.

methods.name: is_set

returns.description: True if the internal flag is set.

methods.description: Return True if and only if the internal flag is true.

classes.name: Semaphore
classes.description: A semaphore manages an internal counter which is decremented by each acquire() call and incremented by each release() call. The counter can never go below zero; acquire() blocks when it would.

params.name: value
params.description: The initial value of the internal counter. Defaults to 1.

constructors.description: Create a semaphore with the given initial value.

methods.name: acquire

params.name: blocking
params.description: If True (default), block until the counter is greater than zero. If False, return immediately.

returns.description: True if acquired, False if non-blocking and the counter was zero.

methods.description: Acquire the semaphore. If the internal counter is greater than zero, decrement it by one and return True. If zero and blocking, block until another thread calls release().

methods.name: release
methods.description: Release the semaphore, incrementing the internal counter by one. May wake up a thread waiting to acquire.

classes.name: BoundedSemaphore
classes.description: A bounded semaphore that checks that the internal counter never exceeds its initial value. If it would, ValueError is raised. Helps catch programming errors where release() is called too many times.

params.name: value
params.description: The initial (and maximum) value of the internal counter. Defaults to 1.

constructors.description: Create a bounded semaphore with the given initial value.

methods.name: acquire

params.name: blocking
params.description: If True (default), block until the counter is greater than zero. If False, return immediately.

returns.description: True if acquired, False if non-blocking and the counter was zero.

methods.description: Acquire the semaphore. Same as Semaphore.acquire().

methods.name: release
methods.description: Release the semaphore. Raises ValueError if this would increase the counter above its initial value.

classes.name: Timer
classes.description: A thread that executes a function after a specified interval has passed. A subclass of Thread.

params.name: interval
params.description: The number of seconds to wait before executing the function.

params.name: function
params.description: The function to call after the interval.

params.name: args
params.description: Arguments to pass to the function. Defaults to [].

params.name: kwargs
params.description: Keyword arguments to pass to the function. Defaults to {}.

constructors.description: Create a timer that will run function with arguments args and keyword arguments kwargs after interval seconds have passed.

methods.name: cancel
methods.description: Stop the timer and cancel the execution of the timer's action. Only works if the timer is still in its waiting stage.
